preamble: The voiceless aspirated retroflex affricate [ʈʂʰ] simplifies to the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts] through deaspiration and merger. This shift involves the loss of both retroflexion and aspiration.

examples.note: Merger of retroflex and alveolar affricates, often accompanied by loss of aspiration contrast in certain dialectal contexts.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
